factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Off (Iran Deal)
factors.summary: Trump announced framework agreement with Iran, canceling military strikes. Markets rallied hard Thursday; Nikkei +4%, South Korea Kospi +8%, gold +3%, oil fell to $85. Dow components (BA, CAT, transports) benefit from reduced supply-chain disruption risk.

factors.name: Hot US PPI Data
factors.summary: US Producer Price Index came in hotter than expected in May, per FXStreet and investinglive reports. This raises input-cost pressure for Dow industrials (CAT, HON, GE) and supports higher-for-longer rates narrative, capping multiple expansion.

factors.name: US Dollar Strength
factors.summary: DXY trades near 99.80, gaining ground on hot PPI and Middle East uncertainty. Strong USD headwind for Dow's ~50% international revenue exposure (MSFT, GE, CAT, BA); multinational translation drag.

factors.name: Oil Price Stabilization
factors.summary: WTI steadies near $85 after Trump's Iran deal comments. Lower energy costs benefit transportation/logistics (UPS, FDX not in Dow but signals cost relief), but Chevron (CVX) in Dow sees margin compression; net modest tailwind given Dow's light energy weighting.

factors.name: Copper & Industrial Metals Rebound
factors.summary: Copper rallied from 3-week low after Trump signaled Iran deal. Signals improved demand outlook for CAT, CMI, industrial construction; easing recession fears among Dow cyclicals.

factors.name: Rate Expectations / Fed Policy
factors.summary: No new FOMC data in window, but hot PPI raises probability of held rates longer. ECB hiked rates first time since 2023, supporting dollar. 10-year yield stable; no major shift to confirm dovish repricing yet.

factors.name: Deal Reality Check / Iran Skepticism
factors.summary: Multiple reports flag Iran Supreme Leader hasn't blessed deal, IRGC rhetoric remains combative, Hormuz tanker reportedly blocked. Trump's 39th 'deal' announcement history. De-escalation narrative faces credibility risk if deal falls apart over weekend.

factors.name: Corporate Profit Cycle & Earnings Resilience
factors.summary: Bloomberg report: corporate profits hit record highs vs. India economy; companies upbeat on demand and capex despite stock struggles. Dow's heavyweight earnings (UNH, GS, JPM, HD, V) historically stable; supports defensive holding.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO / Tech Spectacle Momentum
factors.summary: SpaceX $75B IPO (largest ever) and Nasdaq 100 additions (Rocket Lab, Astera, CoreWeave, AI names) generating retail/foreign investor flows. Positive sentiment spillover to tech-adjacent Dow names (MSFT, AAPL proximity), though Dow itself is industrials-heavy.

factors.name: Adobe CFO Exit / Execution Concerns
factors.summary: Adobe loses second top exec; stock at 7-year low. Signals management/strategy uncertainty in high-growth names; may spill into broader tech/software confidence, indirectly affecting MSFT sentiment.

screening.narrative: The Dow rallied +1.25% in 24h on Iran deal hopes and geopolitical de-escalation, with Nikkei and Kospi surging harder on reduced Middle East supply-chain risk. However, the rally is already ~90min into Phase 2 execution, and hot US PPI data is creating a countercurrent by reinforcing higher-for-longer rates and raising input-cost pressures on Dow industrials. Dollar strength (DXY 99.80) is a headwind for multinational revenue translation. The deal remains fragile—Iran's Supreme Leader has not officially blessed it, and IRGC rhetoric suggests leverage-building rather than surrender, raising overnight-to-weekend flip risk.
catalysts_ahead: Iran deal signing claim/confirmation (weekend, high flip risk)
PBOC monetary policy and yuan management (ongoing, China demand proxy)
RBA decision June 16 (holds expected; forward guidance on inflation/rates)
BOK rate decision next month (Shin hinting hikes; supports KRW, rate-sensitive Dow plays)
Dow component earnings: major banks (JPM, GS), healthcare (UNH), retail (HD) in coming weeks
US CPI/PCE inflation data (next releases post-PPI; rate-sensitive equities pivot point)
ISM Services PMI (mid-June; Dow cyclical confidence)
screening.risk_warning: Iran deal collapse or escalation (drone interception reported, Hormuz blockade threat) would reverse de-escalation rally and spike oil/VIX, hammering Dow's industrial leaders. Dollar unwinding on deal peace-and-calm could flip the headwind. Hot PPI repeat could force higher-rates repricing, capping multiples on dividend/value stocks (banks, utilities). Dow component earnings misses or guidance cuts would derail earnings resilience narrative.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already in PHASE 2 (rally underway ~18h into catalyst); avoid chasing. Wait for pullback to 50,600–50,700 support or a close above 51,000 on Iran deal confirmation before adding. If deal falters overnight, 50,400–50,500 becomes tactical entry for reversal.

factors.name: Iran deal optimism & oil crash
factors.summary: Trump announced framework agreement with Iran; Brent crude fell below $90/bbl on ceasefire hopes and Hormuz reopening prospects (Societe Generale, Deutsche Bank, ING reports). Lower energy input costs and reduced inflation shock support equities and reduce Fed hike urgency.

factors.name: Hot US PPI inflation data
factors.summary: US PPI came in hotter than expected (mentioned in NZD/USD and Canadian Dollar reports); this contradicts the dovish tilt from oil collapse and could extend Fed tightening cycle. Core inflation pressures persist.

factors.name: ECB hawkish hold + July optionality
factors.summary: ECB hiked 25bps and signalled readiness for second hike in July if energy shock persists (Nagel, Makhlouf). While markets price two more hikes, ECB avoided clear guidance (Commerzbank), creating uncertainty. Euro rally (EUR/USD to 1.1600) signals some risk-off easing but Fed path remains uncertain.

factors.name: S&P 500 strong rally, largest in 2 months
factors.summary: Deutsche Bank noted S&P 500 posted largest gain in two months on lower oil + reduced Fed hike expectations. Risk sentiment has improved markedly from geopolitical fears; broader equity momentum favours US30 participation.

factors.name: Dow 4h/24h price action already up +0.52%/+1.25%
factors.summary: Rally is already underway; move has not extended dramatically but is material. Suggests Phase 2 consolidation or mild pullback likely before further acceleration. Momentum intact but near-term overhead building.

factors.name: UK GDP -0.1% m/m (weak), services slump
factors.summary: UK economy marginally contracted in April as services sector cooled. Signals developed-market growth fragility; less relevant to US30 directly but feeds global risk narrative if spread continues.

factors.name: Dollar strength (DXY ~99.80) on hawkish inflation
factors.summary: USD Index holding elevated levels despite Iran deal relief, driven by hot US PPI and reduced Fed dovish bets. Strong dollar historically headwind for Dow multinationals (~50% international revenue); translation drag likely.

factors.name: SpaceX blockbuster IPO (+35% shadow trading indicated)
factors.summary: Historic $75B IPO launches today; broad retail/institutional enthusiasm boosts sentiment and liquidity. However, mega-cap IPO tail-risk (volatility, capital rotation from dividend Dow names) is real. Musk mania may elevate tech over cyclicals.

factors.name: Core inflation edging up in EU (Germany +2.5%, France +2.4%, Spain +3.0%)
factors.summary: European core inflation accelerating despite oil relief, suggesting secondary effects from energy shock are broadening. Supports ECB's hawkish tilt and complicates growth outlook. Negative for global cyclical demand and Dow industrials exposure.

factors.name: Fed forward guidance and rate cut expectations
factors.summary: No fresh Fed communication in this window; prior messaging saw rate hikes fading on oil collapse but hot PPI has relit some hawkishness. Kevin Warsh (incoming Chair) not yet signalled policy direction. Ambiguity caps confidence.

factors.name: Dow heavyweight earnings & guidance absent
factors.summary: No recent earnings surprises or forward guidance from major Dow components (UNH, MSFT, GS, CAT, JPM, BA) in this window. Earnings calendar opacity limits conviction on cyclical outlook.

factors.name: China growth data (PBOC CNY setting, general Asia momentum)
factors.summary: PBOC set USD/CNY reference at 6.8109 vs 6.7640 estimate (CNY slightly weaker, neutral). No China PMI or growth data in window; Asia equities rallied on Iran deal but no fundamental China refresh on CAT/DE demand drivers.

key_levels.support: 50,600 (psychological, recent swing low near June 11 close)

screening.narrative: US30 is riding a relief rally on Iran deal optimism and oil-price collapse (Brent <$90), which eases inflation/stagflation fears and loosens Fed hike expectations. S&P 500 posted its biggest gain in 2 months on this backdrop. However, the move is already +1.25% in 24h (Phase 2), and hot US PPI data plus ECB hawkish-hold messaging re-introduce rate and growth uncertainty. SpaceX's blockbuster IPO today adds sentiment lift but also tail risks of capital rotation away from dividend-weighted Dow. Dollar strength persists despite oil relief, headwind for multinational translation. Near-term consolidation likely before next directional move.
catalysts_ahead: SpaceX IPO opening today (sentiment driver, potential retail inflows vs rotation from Dow)
RBA decision June 15–16 (likely hold; guidance on inflation/hikes critical for AUD/USD and risk sentiment)
US economic calendar: CPI/PCE, durable goods, factory orders in coming days (could reignite Fed tightening debate if hot)
Iran deal finalization or collapse (binary event; no ceasefire = oil re-spike, risk-off, Dow weakness)
ECB July meeting (July 3–4) — ECB signalled readiness for second hike; market expectations vs reality could move risk assets
Fed speakers (Kevin Warsh, Powell, other governors) — incoming Chair's first signals on policy path critical
screening.risk_warning: If Iran deal collapses or stalls, oil spikes back above $100, triggering stagflation fears and sharp equity selloff (Dow down 2–3%). If US PPI/CPI surprise to the hot side next week, Fed hawkishness re-emerges and rate-cut expectations collapse, crashing dividend/cyclical names and Dow hard. If SpaceX IPO flops or triggers sector rotation away from industrials/financials, momentum breaks. If China growth disappoints, CAT/BA/DE earnings guidance weakens, cyclical confidence erodes.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (+1.25% in 24h); wait for consolidation/pullback toward 50,600–50,700 before fresh long entries. If break above 51,100 on volume, trail stops tight and take profits into resistance. Avoid chasing into SpaceX euphoria; focus on Dow-specific drivers (earnings, Fed path, dollar strength).

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal (Geopolitical De-risking)
factors.summary: US-Iran MoU framework nearing signature as soon as Sunday in Geneva per Bloomberg/IRNA. De-escalation reduces Middle East tail risk, eases oil-price defensive positioning, and supports risk-on sentiment for cyclical equities like Dow industrials.

factors.name: Oil Price Decline
factors.summary: WTI trading ~$82.90, down for second consecutive day, as Hormuz risk premium unwinding. Lower energy input costs benefit Dow transport/industrial names (CVX excepted as energy producer); reduces inflation-carry headwind.

factors.name: Fed Policy Stance (Warsh Era Begins)
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh's first FOMC on 17 June expected to stay cautious per Nordea; earlier rate-cut projections likely removed from dot plot, some hike calls appearing. Market expects 18 bps hikes by year-end (97% no-hike probability at next meeting). Hawkish surprise risk capped near-term.

factors.name: Dollar Strength & Real Yields
factors.summary: USD stable as US growth outperforms Eurozone; ECB hiked but Lagarde hawkish commentary already digested. MUFG notes higher-for-longer USD backdrop on resilient inflation & labour. Neutral for Dow given 50% international revenue but offsets weakness in emerging markets (INR, BRL rallying on deal hopes).

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Mega-Event ($75B Record)
factors.summary: Nasdaq debut Friday with shadow-market trading showing ~35% implied pop per Bloomberg. Record IPO issuance boosting investment-bank trading revenue (JPMorgan notes GS, MS upside); psychologically risk-on for growth/tech-adjacent sentiment, spillover bullish for broad equity rotation.

factors.name: Equity Futures Rally Pre-Open
factors.summary: S&P 500 futures +0.6% as of 7:36 ET per Bloomberg, propelled by Iran deal hopes and SpaceX listing. Dow 24h change +1.25%, 4h +0.52%, confirming intra-session momentum into cash open Friday.

factors.name: Credit De-risking (Europe)
factors.summary: Credit traders unwinding $20B wartime short in European corporates per Bloomberg, signalling reduced geopolitical hedging demand and renewed appetite for cyclical credit — supportive for IG-heavy Dow financials & industrials.

factors.name: Inflation Data (India CPI 3.93% YoY May)
factors.summary: India CPI 3.93% vs 4.0% estimate, lower than 3.48% prior. RBI inflation under control, supporting rupee recovery. US Michigan Consumer Sentiment Index expected weak in June per FXStreet, but no hot US inflation print in this news window to derail macro outlook.

factors.name: China Credit Growth Slowdown
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports China credit expansion slowed May YoY despite regulator push for lending; weak private demand persists. Headwind for Dow heavies with China exposure (CAT, BA, GE), but offset by de-escalation narrative.

factors.name: Dow Component Earnings & Guidance Risk
factors.summary: DA Davidson cuts Adobe price target on ARR concerns; no major Dow 30 earnings surprises flagged in this window. Awaiting next cycle of earnings guidance (CAT, DE, GE, HON) for cyclical health confirmation.

factors.name: 10Y Treasury Yield / Rate Expectations
factors.summary: No major Treasury move flagged in news. Fed expectations anchored at ~18 bps hikes YE; Warsh debut 17 June will be key test of hawkish/neutral tone. No clear downside yield shock to drive dividend rally yet.

factors.name: VIX / Market Volatility
factors.summary: Bloomberg notes 'extreme rotations leave stock market bulls without a playbook' — volatility has spiked on positioning swings, but Iran deal narrows tail risk. No VIX level cited; likely moderating intra-day.

screening.narrative: Dow is rallying on a confluence of de-risking themes: US-Iran peace deal nearing signature, oil prices falling, credit hedges unwinding, and SpaceX's historic $75B IPO lifting risk sentiment and investment-bank earnings outlook. Fed Warsh debut on 17 June looms as potential hawkish wildcard, but near-term momentum favours 'risk-on' as geopolitical tail-risk premium collapses. However, China credit slowdown and lack of fresh US economic surprises (ISM, durable goods not yet released this week) leave upside slightly exposed if euphoria fades.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran MoU formal signature (expected Sunday in Geneva)
SpaceX Nasdaq opening trade (Friday 12 June)
Michigan Consumer Sentiment Index preliminary (Friday 12 June)
Kevin Warsh first FOMC meeting (17 June) — major communications test
ISM Manufacturing/Services PMI (likely mid-next week) — Dow cyclical health check
Durable goods orders, industrial production (mid-June data)
China June PMI data (early July) — long-term cyclical signal
screening.risk_warning: Iran deal collapse or last-minute delays could reverse euphoria instantly. SpaceX pop fizzles / rotation reverses if mega-cap tech underperforms. Warsh FOMC surprise on 17 June with hawkish hike signals could trigger correction. China slowdown data (credit, PMI) worsening could undermine cyclical Dow thesis. Geopolitical flare-up (Israel/Lebanon escalation per Al Jazeera reports) could reignite risk-off. Entry too late into this move risks mean-reversion pullback.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already underway (+1.25% in 24h). Consider adding on pullback to 50,600–50,750 support (retest of Thursday lows) before re-engaging. If breaks above 51,100 on SpaceX pop, watch for exhaustion / profit-taking into European close before fresh longs. Avoid chasing on open Friday; wait for 60-90 min price action stabilization post-SpaceX listing.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal Progress
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports US-Iran MoU could be signed Sunday in Geneva, potentially reopening Strait of Hormuz. IRNA confirms framework text nearly finalized. Major geopolitical de-escalation reduces risk premium, supports risk-on sentiment for equities.

factors.name: Oil Price Decline
factors.summary: WTI fell to ~$82.90 on Iran deal hopes (MarketWatch reports oil fell on potential sanctions lift). Lower energy input costs benefit Dow industrials (CAT, MMM, HON, BA) and improve margin outlooks for manufacturing-heavy components.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Euphoria
factors.summary: Record $75B SpaceX IPO debuts Friday on Nasdaq, creating broad market enthusiasm. Bloomberg reports S&P 500 futures up 0.6% pre-market. JPMorgan notes mega-IPO activity generates strong trading income for Dow financials (GS, JPM) and supports broader risk appetite.

factors.name: Equity Market Momentum
factors.summary: US30 already up +1.25% in 24h and +0.52% in 4h on Iran breakthrough. FXStreet reports futures extending rally. Momentum is positive but move is maturing - Phase 2 scenario with partial gains already captured.

factors.name: Fed Policy Uncertainty (Warsh Debut)
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh's first FOMC meeting June 17 approaching. Nordea expects more neutral stance with rate cut projections removed from dot plot. MarketWatch notes no communications blackout expected. Creates near-term ceiling on conviction until meeting clarity.

factors.name: US Dollar Dynamics
factors.summary: MUFG notes higher-for-longer rates backdrop supports USD, but BBH reports USD weakened initially on Iran news. For Dow's 50% international revenue exposure, current DXY stability is neutral - not providing strong headwind or tailwind.

factors.name: India Economic Stress
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports India shifting to crisis mode with fuel curbs and wider deficit due to Iran war energy costs. Signals emerging market stress and potential demand destruction for Dow industrial exports (CAT construction equipment, BA aircraft).

factors.name: European Auto Alliance
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports VW, Stellantis, Renault pushing 'Made in Europe' plans to counter China. Indirectly competitive pressure on global industrials, but not directly material to US30 components in near term.

factors.name: Credit Market Positioning
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports credit traders unwinding $20B wartime short in Europe. Risk-off hedges being removed supports broader equity rally as geopolitical premium compressed.

factors.name: Sector Rotation Signals
factors.summary: Bloomberg notes 'extreme rotations' leaving bulls without playbook. Suggests unstable internal market dynamics even as headline indices rise - increases probability of whipsaw reversals.

factors.name: China Credit Growth Slowdown
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports China credit expansion slowed in May, private demand subdued despite regulatory push. Negative for Dow heavy exporters to China (BA, CAT) and signals weak external demand for US industrials.

factors.name: Upcoming Michigan Sentiment
factors.summary: FXStreet notes UoM Consumer Sentiment expected to remain weak in June amid elevated inflation. Release due Friday - potential near-term volatility catalyst but not yet known.

confirmation.narrative: US30 is extending its risk-on rally (+1.25% in 24h) on breakthrough US-Iran peace deal momentum, with MoU expected Sunday in Geneva per Bloomberg. The geopolitical de-escalation is compressing risk premiums, driving oil down to $82.90 which benefits Dow's industrial-heavy composition. SpaceX's record $75B IPO is adding euphoria, with JPMorgan noting strong trading income tailwinds for Dow financials. However, the move is Phase 2 - substantial gains already captured - and Warsh's June 17 FOMC debut creates a near-term uncertainty ceiling. China credit slowdown and India crisis signals present medium-term demand headwinds for cyclical exporters.
catalysts_ahead: Michigan Consumer Sentiment (Friday, imminent)
FOMC meeting June 17 (Warsh debut, dot plot revision expected)
US-Iran MoU signing Sunday in Geneva
SpaceX trading debut Friday (sentiment spillover)
Dow component earnings season (upcoming Q2 reports)
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidates if US-Iran deal collapses (oil spike, risk-off), Warsh FOMC turns unexpectedly hawkish on June 17, or China data deteriorates sharply signaling global demand destruction. Immediate risk: move already extended +1.25%, vulnerable to profit-taking if Michigan Sentiment disappoints or if Iran deal details underwhelm market expectations.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move is Phase 2 with gains already captured; consider waiting for pullback to 50400-50500 support zone before adding long exposure, or use tight stops above 50850 if chasing current levels into weekend geopolitical event risk.

screening.current_price: ~43,500 (estimated; intraday weakness but off lows after UMich data)

factors.name: US Consumer Sentiment (UMich Flash)
factors.summary: UMich Consumer Sentiment Index rose to 48.9 in June vs. 46.0 expected and 44.8 in May. Current conditions 48.4 vs. 46.2 exp, expectations 49.3 vs. 44.3 exp. First improvement in 4 months; inflation expectations also eased (1-year 4.6% vs 4.8% prior, 5-year 3.4% vs 3.9% prior). This is a material surprise-to-upside that supports consumer spending and Dow dividend payers.

factors.name: Iran Peace Deal Chatter / De-escalation Risk-Off
factors.summary: Earlier bullish sentiment on US-Iran ceasefire waned sharply after Trump called leaked terms 'fake' and 'dishonorable.' Oil prices fell to $88/bbl on peace hopes but reversed; geopolitical uncertainty remains. Risk-off sentiment favors defensive rotation, pressuring cyclical Dow heavyweights (CAT, BA, industrials).

factors.name: SpaceX IPO / Retail Capital Demand
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO opened at ~$150–172 vs. $135 IPO, drawing massive retail FOMO and capital deployment. While sentiment-bullish, Citi notes 'broad-based demand for capital' across IPOs/secondaries may pull liquidity from broader equity markets. S&P 500 excluded SpaceX from index, limiting fund flows.

factors.name: Tech Sector Strength (AMD +5.39%, NVDA +0.54%, TXN +1.14%)
factors.summary: Semiconductor rally driven by AMD's AI chip momentum (Citi sees Meta spending underestimated). However, software stocks tumbled; sector breadth mixed. Dow exposure to Tech is limited vs. SPY, muting impact.

factors.name: Fed Policy & Rate Expectations
factors.summary: No new Fed action in news window. ECB delivered 25bp hike to 2.25% depo rate with hawkish guidance; inflation risks cited in Europe. US 10-year and long yields not explicitly highlighted as moving; likely consolidating. No major near-term FOMC signal.

factors.name: Energy / Oil Prices
factors.summary: WTI fell to $88/bbl on Iran peace hopes; ~50% of Hormuz stoppages restored per Energy Secretary Wright. Lower energy costs help consumers but weigh on CVX and energy input cost narrative. Transportation costs ease, margin-positive for industrials, but sentiment-negative on energy demand outlook.

factors.name: Dollar Strength (DXY Holding Firm)
factors.summary: DXY maintaining support despite peace-deal chatter softening USD in near term. No major FX shock; multinational Dow names (BA, CAT, GS, JPM) seeing mixed translation effects. Risk of further USD weakness if Iran deal confirmed, pressuring dollar-denominated exports.

factors.name: Dow Heavyweight Earnings & Guidance
factors.summary: Adobe CFO departure and resignation of CEO cited as negative signal on tech/software stability. No earnings surprises from core Dow industrial leaders (CAT, DE, HON, BA, GE, JPM, UNH) disclosed in window. Next earnings season catalyst not imminent.

factors.name: Market Breadth & Intraday Action
factors.summary: Broader US indices lower on day despite UMich beat; rally off lows suggests chop and lack of conviction. Investors torn between consumer sentiment strength and geopolitical uncertainty. Software weakness and cyclical pressure offset by defensive rebound.

factors.name: China Growth / Cyclical Demand Signals
factors.summary: No China PMI or growth data in news window. Cyclical leaders (CAT, BA, materials) exposed to China slowdown risk; no fresh catalysts. Uncertainty on trade/tariff policy under current admin.

key_levels.support: ~43,200 (intraday low off risk-off on Iran deal confusion)

screening.narrative: The Dow opened with cyclical headwinds from Iran deal confusion and broad-market risk-off, but the University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment surprise-beat (48.9 vs. 46.0) provided a floor and sparked a recovery off intraday lows. UMich's improvement to a 4-month high, combined with easing inflation expectations, supports the consumer spending narrative that underpins Dow dividend payers and financials (JPM, GS, HD, UNH). However, the enthusiasm is tempered by SpaceX IPO capital drain, software weakness, and renewed geopolitical uncertainty after Trump disputed Iran deal terms. The Dow is in a Phase 2 / early Phase 3 consolidation: the morning weakness has bounced, but breadth remains mixed, and no high-conviction cycle catalyst (earnings, Fed, major China data) is fresh.
catalysts_ahead: Iran deal finalization (Trump mentioned signing 'this weekend' in Europe; remains uncertain)
ECB next meeting / further rate guidance (hawkish tone locked in Friday)
UK BoE decision next week + CPI/labour data (GBP volatility, spillover to US sentiment)
Dow heavyweight earnings backlog (UNH, JPM, GS, HD, BA guidance sessions coming in coming weeks)
ISM Manufacturing & Services PMI (June data, likely next week; industrial composition critical for US30)
Durable goods, factory orders, industrial production (June releases TBD; leading indicators for CAT, BA, GE)
Fed speakers / rate-cut expectations (market pricing ~2 cuts in 2026; no Fed action Friday but rhetoric will matter)
screening.risk_warning: If Iran deal collapses or re-escalates (new attack, Trump walks away), risk-off will crush cyclical industrials and financials, pushing Dow lower. If UMich strength is validated by retail sales & housing data next week, and Fed delays cuts, equities rally. If SpaceX IPO creates persistent liquidity drain or credit stress (bond yields spike on capital demand), that would invert the BUY bias. Finally, if China PMI disappoints or trade war rhetoric intensifies, CAT, BA, and other Dow heavies will face headwinds.
screening.entry_guidance: Dow bouncing off intraday lows on UMich surprise; wait for retest of ~43,400–43,500 support to confirm hold before adding longs. Resistance at ~43,800–44,000. If Iran deal headlines deteriorate further, expect break of support and Phase 3 selloff; consider shorting breaks of ~43,200 on intraday weakness. Move currently mid-phase and choppy; do not chase rips into tech-led rallies—wait for industrial / cyclical names to confirm upside.
